Name: Ayushman Bharat - Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY)
SubHead: A flagship scheme of Government of India to provide health cover of Rs. 5 lakhs per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization.
Desc:
Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY) is the world's largest health insurance/assurance scheme fully financed by the government. It provides a cover of Rs. 5 lakhs per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization across public and private empanelled hospitals in India.

Cashless access to health care services for the beneficiary at the point of service. It covers up to 3 days of pre-hospitalization and 15 days post-hospitalization expenses such as diagnostics and medicines. There is no restriction on the family size, age or gender. All pre-existing conditions are covered from day one.

Name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
SubHead: A central sector scheme to supplement the financial needs of all land-holding farmers.
Desc:
To supplement the financial needs of the Small and Marginal Farmers in procuring various inputs to ensure proper crop health and appropriate yields, commensurate with the anticipated farm income.

Under the scheme, an income support of Rs. 6,000 per year in three equal installments is provided to all land holding farmer families. The fund is directly transferred to the bank accounts of the beneficiaries.

Name: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY)
SubHead: A scheme to make clean cooking fuel such as LPG available to the rural and deprived households.
Desc:
To provide clean cooking fuel (LPG) to women from poor households, protecting their health from the hazardous effects of traditional cooking fuels like firewood, coal, and cow-dung cakes.

Eligible women from BPL families receive a deposit-free LPG connection. The connection includes a cylinder and a regulator. The government provides a subsidy on each cylinder.

Name: Stand-Up India Scheme
SubHead: Facilitating bank loans for setting up a greenfield enterprise by SC/ST and women entrepreneurs.
Desc:
To promote entrepreneurship among women and Scheduled Castes and Tribes. The scheme is anchored by the Department of Financial Services (DFS), Ministry of Finance, Government of India.

Facilitates bank loans between Rs. 10 lakh and Rs. 1 Crore to at least one Scheduled Caste (SC) or Scheduled Tribe (ST) borrower and at least one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up a greenfield enterprise in manufacturing, services, or the trading sector.

Name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
SubHead: A pension scheme for citizens of India focused on the unorganized sector workers.
Desc:
To create a universal social security system for all Indians, especially the poor, the under-privileged and the workers in the unorganized sector. It is administered by the Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA).

Provides a guaranteed minimum monthly pension of Rs. 1,000, Rs. 2,000, Rs. 3,000, Rs. 4,000 or Rs. 5,000 at the age of 60 years, based on the contributions made by the subscriber.

Name: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY)
SubHead: A skill development initiative scheme of the Government of India for recognition and standardization of skills.
Desc:
To enable a large number of Indian youth to take up industry-relevant skill training that will help them in securing a better livelihood. Individuals with prior learning experience or skills will also be assessed and certified under the Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL) component of the Scheme.

Provides free skill training, assessment, and certification. Financial reward and placement assistance are also provided to successful candidates.

Name: Scholarship for Students with Disabilities (Post-Matric)
SubHead: Financial assistance for students with disabilities for studies from Class 11 to Master's level.
Desc:
To support students with disabilities for their post-matriculation education. The scheme aims to enable them to complete their education and become self-reliant.

The scheme provides financial assistance for tuition fees, maintenance allowance, book grants, and disability allowances.

Name: Skill Loan Scheme
SubHead: Provides loans up to Rs. 1,50,000 for students pursuing skill development courses.
Desc:
To provide a loan facility to individuals who intend to take up skill development courses as per the Skilling Framework. The scheme aims to support the national skilling mission.

Provides need-based finance to cover tuition fees and other course-related expenses. Loan amounts range from Rs. 5,000 to Rs. 1,50,000. No collateral is required.

Name: Women Scientists Scheme-C (WOS-C)
SubHead: Offers training in Intellectual Property Rights (IPR) for women with a career break.
Desc:
To train women having qualifications in science/engineering/medicine or allied areas in the field of Intellectual Property Rights (IPRs) and their management, for a period of one year. The scheme aims to create a pool of women scientists knowledgeable in IPR.

Provides a one-year training program on IPR. A monthly stipend is given to the trainees.

Name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
SubHead: A one-year life insurance scheme, renewable from year to year, offering coverage for death due to any reason.
Desc:
To provide life insurance cover to all citizens, especially the poor and underprivileged sections of society.

Provides a life cover of Rs. 2 Lakhs in case of death of the insured person due to any reason. The premium is very low, at Rs. 436 per annum.

Name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
SubHead: A one-year accidental death and disability cover, renewable from year to year.
Desc:
To provide affordable insurance cover against accidental death and disability.

Provides a cover of Rs. 2 Lakhs for accidental death or total and permanent disability, and Rs. 1 Lakh for permanent partial disability. The premium is extremely low at Rs. 20 per annum.

Name: National Pension Scheme for Traders and Self-Employed Persons (NPS-Traders)
SubHead: A pension scheme for retail traders, shopkeepers, and self-employed persons.
Desc:
To provide old age protection and social security for retail traders, shopkeepers, and self-employed persons whose annual turnover is not exceeding Rs 1.5 crore.

It is a voluntary and contributory pension scheme under which the subscriber would receive a minimum assured pension of Rs 3000 per month after attaining the age of 60 years. The Government of India makes a matching contribution to the subscriber's account.

Name: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA)
SubHead: Aims to enhance livelihood security in rural areas by providing at least 100 days of wage employment.
Desc:
To enhance livelihood security in rural areas by providing at least 100 days of guaranteed wage employment in a financial year to every household whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work.

Guaranteed 100 days of employment. Creation of durable assets (such as roads, canals, ponds, wells). Social inclusion of women, SCs, and STs.

Name: National Food Security Mission (NFSM)
SubHead: Aims to increase the production of rice, wheat, pulses, coarse cereals, and commercial crops.
Desc:
To increase production of rice, wheat, pulses, coarse cereals (maize and barley) and commercial crops through area expansion and productivity enhancement in a sustainable manner in the identified districts of the country.

Provides subsidies for seeds, machinery, and other inputs. Promotes improved farming practices and technologies.

Name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY)
SubHead: A scheme to bring about a Blue Revolution through sustainable and responsible development of the fisheries sector.
Desc:
To bring about a Blue Revolution through sustainable and responsible development of the fisheries sector in India. It aims to enhance fish production and productivity, modernize the value chain, and ensure the socio-economic welfare of fishers and fish farmers.

Provides financial assistance for various activities like construction of new ponds, establishment of hatcheries, and adoption of new technologies. It also includes provisions for insurance cover for fishers.

Name: Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM)
SubHead: Aims to reduce poverty by enabling poor households to access gainful self-employment and skilled wage employment opportunities.
Desc:
To reduce poverty by enabling the poor households to access gainful self-employment and skilled wage employment opportunities, resulting in appreciable improvement in their livelihoods on a sustainable basis, through building strong grassroots institutions of the poor.

Promotes the formation of Self-Help Groups (SHGs). Provides financial assistance in the form of Revolving Fund and Community Investment Fund to SHGs. Facilitates access to bank credit.

Name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Gramin (PMAY-G)
SubHead: A rural housing scheme with the objective to provide a pucca house with basic amenities to all houseless households.
Desc:
To provide a pucca house with basic amenities to all houseless households and those households living in kutcha and dilapidated houses in rural areas by 2024.

Provides financial assistance for the construction of a pucca house. The assistance is transferred directly to the beneficiary's bank account.

Name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U)
SubHead: An urban housing scheme with the mission of Housing for All in urban areas.
Desc:
To address the urban housing shortage among the EWS/LIG and MIG categories including the slum dwellers by ensuring a pucca house to all eligible urban households by the year 2022.

The scheme has four components: In-situ Slum Redevelopment (ISSR), Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme (CLSS), Affordable Housing in Partnership (AHP), and Beneficiary-led individual house construction/enhancement (BLC).

Name: Swachh Bharat Mission - Gramin (SBM-G)
SubHead: A mission to achieve a clean and Open Defecation Free (ODF) India in rural areas.
Desc:
To achieve a clean and Open Defecation Free (ODF) India by 2nd October 2019, the 150th birth anniversary of Mahatma Gandhi. The focus is on behavior change and construction of individual household latrines.

Provides an incentive for the construction of Individual Household Latrines (IHHL). Focuses on solid and liquid waste management activities in rural areas.

Name: Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM)
SubHead: A mission to provide safe and adequate drinking water through individual household tap connections to all households in rural India.
Desc:
To provide safe and adequate drinking water through individual household tap connections (Functional Household Tap Connection - FHTC) to all households in rural India by 2024.

Ensures piped water supply to every rural household. Focuses on water conservation, rainwater harvesting, and rejuvenation of traditional water bodies.

Name: National Health Mission (NHM)
SubHead: A mission to provide accessible, affordable and quality health care to the rural population, especially the vulnerable groups.
Desc:
To provide accessible, affordable and quality health care to the rural population, especially the vulnerable groups. It encompasses two sub-missions, the National Rural Health Mission (NRHM) and the National Urban Health Mission (NUHM).

Strengthens health infrastructure, provides free essential drugs and diagnostics, and supports reproductive, maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health (RMNCH+A) services.

Name: Samagra Shiksha Abhiyan
SubHead: An integrated scheme for school education extending from pre-school to class 12.
Desc:
To ensure inclusive and equitable quality education at all levels of school education. It subsumes the three erstwhile schemes of Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan (SSA), Rashtriya Madhyamik Shiksha Abhiyan (RMSA), and Teacher Education (TE).

Provides support for infrastructure development, teacher salaries, training, free textbooks, uniforms, and interventions for inclusive education for children with special needs.

Name: Mid Day Meal Scheme (PM POSHAN)
SubHead: A scheme to enhance the nutritional status of school-age children nationwide.
Desc:
To enhance the enrollment, retention, and attendance of children in schools and simultaneously improve their nutritional levels. The scheme has been renamed PM POSHAN Shakti Nirman.

Provides one hot cooked meal to all children studying in Classes I-VIII in Government, Government-aided schools, Special Training Centres (STC) and Madarsas & Maqtabs supported under Samagra Shiksha.

Name: Beti Bachao Beti Padhao Scheme
SubHead: A scheme to address the declining Child Sex Ratio (CSR) and related issues of women empowerment.
Desc:
To prevent gender-biased sex selective elimination, ensure survival & protection of the girl child, and ensure education and participation of the girl child.

It is primarily a social campaign and does not involve direct cash transfer. It focuses on awareness campaigns, and multi-sectoral action in districts with low Child Sex Ratio.

Name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
SubHead: A small deposit scheme for the girl child, as part of the 'Beti Bachao Beti Padhao' campaign.
Desc:
To encourage parents to build a fund for the future education and marriage expenses for their female child.

Offers a high rate of interest, which is compounded annually. The contributions and the maturity amount are exempt from tax under Section 80C.

Name: National Means-cum-Merit Scholarship Scheme (NMMSS)
SubHead: A scholarship for meritorious students of economically weaker sections to arrest their drop-out at class VIII.
Desc:
To award scholarships to meritorious students of economically weaker sections to arrest their drop-out at class VIII and encourage them to continue their study at the secondary stage.

Scholarship of Rs. 12,000 per annum is awarded to selected students every year for study in classes from IX to XII in State Government, Government-aided and local body schools.

Name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
SubHead: An actuarial premium based crop insurance scheme to provide financial support to farmers suffering crop loss/damage.
Desc:
To provide insurance coverage and financial support to the farmers in the event of failure of any of the notified crop as a result of natural calamities, pests & diseases.

Provides comprehensive insurance cover against crop loss. The premium paid by farmers is very low (2% for Kharif, 1.5% for Rabi, and 5% for commercial/horticultural crops).

Name: Soil Health Card Scheme
SubHead: A scheme to provide every farmer with a Soil Health Card to help them improve productivity through judicious use of inputs.
Desc:
To issue Soil Health Cards to farmers every 2 years so as to provide a basis to address nutritional deficiencies in fertilization practices. It aims to promote soil test based and balanced use of fertilizers.

The card provides information on the nutrient status of the soil along with recommendations on the appropriate dosage of nutrients to be applied for improving soil health and its fertility.

Name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC) Scheme
SubHead: A scheme to provide adequate and timely credit support from the banking system to farmers.
Desc:
To provide adequate and timely credit support from the banking system under a single window with flexible and simplified procedure to the farmers for their cultivation and other needs.

Provides credit for cultivation expenses, post-harvest expenses, and consumption requirements of the farmer household. The KCC now also covers animal husbandry and fisheries.

Name: Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (PMGSY)
SubHead: A nationwide plan to provide good all-weather road connectivity to unconnected habitations.
Desc:
To provide all-weather access to eligible unconnected habitations in the rural areas of India.

Construction of good quality, all-weather roads connecting rural habitations. This improves access to markets, education, and healthcare for the rural population.

Name: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY)
SubHead: A placement-linked skill training program for rural youth.
Desc:
To transform rural poor youth into an economically independent and globally relevant workforce. It is a part of the National Rural Livelihoods Mission (NRLM).

Provides demand-led, placement-linked skill training free of cost. It has a special focus on socially disadvantaged groups. Post-placement support is also provided.

Name: National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (NAPS)
SubHead: A scheme to promote apprenticeship training and to increase the engagement of apprentices.
Desc:
To promote apprenticeship in the country by providing financial incentives, technology and advocacy support. The scheme aims to increase the number of apprentices hired by employers.

The Government of India shares 25% of the prescribed stipend subject to a maximum of Rs. 1500 per month per apprentice with the employers. The government also shares the cost of basic training with Basic Training Providers.

Name: Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-dhan (PM-SYM)
SubHead: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme for unorganized workers.
Desc:
To provide old age protection and social security for Unorganized Workers (UW) who are mostly engaged as rickshaw pullers, street vendors, mid-day meal workers, head loaders, brick kiln workers, cobblers, rag pickers, domestic workers, washermen, home-based workers, own account workers, agricultural labourers, construction workers, beedi workers, handloom workers, leather workers, audio-visual workers or in similar other occupations.

Each subscriber shall receive a minimum assured pension of Rs 3000 per month after attaining the age of 60 years. The Government of India makes a matching contribution.

Name: Mission for Integrated Development of Horticulture (MIDH)
SubHead: A scheme for the holistic growth of the horticulture sector.
Desc:
To promote holistic growth of the horticulture sector, including fruits, vegetables, root & tuber crops, mushrooms, spices, flowers, aromatic plants, coconut, cashew, cocoa and bamboo.

Provides financial assistance for various interventions like production of planting material, vegetable seed production, cultivation of flowers, development of infrastructure for post-harvest management, etc.

Name: Khelo India Scheme
SubHead: A national programme for development of sports in India.
Desc:
To build a strong framework for all sports played in our country and establish India as a great sporting nation. It aims to promote 'Sports for All' as well as 'Sports for Excellence'.

Provides financial assistance to talented sportspersons, supports the development of sports infrastructure, and organizes annual sports competitions like the Khelo India Youth Games and University Games.

Name: National Action Plan for Senior Citizens (NAPSrC)
SubHead: A plan to ensure the well-being of senior citizens in the country.
Desc:
To create a society where Senior Citizens live a healthy, happy, empowered, dignified and self-reliant life along with strong social and inter-generational bonding. It encompasses various sub-schemes.

Includes schemes like 'Rashtriya Vayoshri Yojana' which provides physical aids and assisted-living devices to senior citizens belonging to BPL category. It also supports the establishment of old age homes.

Name: Pradhan Mantri Jan Vikas Karyakram (PMJVK)
SubHead: A scheme designed to address the development deficits in identified Minority Concentration Areas.
Desc:
To develop socio-economic infrastructure and basic amenities in identified Minority Concentration Areas to bring them at par with the national average. The scheme focuses on education, health, and skill development.

Provides funding for infrastructure projects like schools, colleges, hostels, hospitals, and skill development centers in the identified areas.

Name: Venture Capital Fund for Scheduled Castes
SubHead: A scheme to promote entrepreneurship among the Scheduled Castes who are oriented towards innovation.
Desc:
To promote entrepreneurship among the Scheduled Castes and to provide concessional finance to them. The fund aims to support companies with high growth potential.

Provides financial assistance in the form of equity or loans to SC entrepreneurs. The fund size is managed by IFCI Venture Capital Funds Ltd.

Name: Credit Guarantee Fund Scheme for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE)
SubHead: A scheme to make available collateral-free credit to the micro and small enterprise sector.
Desc:
To make available collateral-free credit to the micro and small enterprise sector. Both the existing and the new enterprises are eligible to be covered under the scheme.

Provides a credit guarantee cover for loans up to Rs. 2 crore extended by lending institutions to Micro and Small Enterprises without any collateral security.

Name: National Scheme of Incentive to Girls for Secondary Education (NSIGSE)
SubHead: A scheme to promote enrollment of girl children in the age group of 14-18 at the secondary stage.
Desc:
To establish an enabling environment to reduce the drop-outs and to promote the enrollment of girl children, especially from SC/ST communities, in secondary schools.

A sum of Rs. 3000 is deposited as a fixed deposit in the name of eligible unmarried girls. They are entitled to withdraw it along with interest thereon on reaching 18 years of age and passing the 10th class examination.

Name: Digital India Programme
SubHead: A flagship programme of the Government of India with a vision to transform India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy.
Desc:
To transform India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy. The programme is centered on three key vision areas: Digital Infrastructure as a Core Utility to Every Citizen, Governance & Services on Demand, and Digital Empowerment of Citizens.

Has led to the creation of various digital platforms and services like Aadhaar, DigiLocker, UMANG, MyGov, and the expansion of Common Service Centres (CSCs). It promotes digital literacy and access to digital services.

Name: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS)
SubHead: A scheme to provide financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, market entry, and commercialization.
Desc:
To provide financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, market entry, and commercialization. It aims to support startups at the very early stage of their journey.

Provides grants up to Rs. 20 Lakhs for validation of Proof of Concept, or prototype development. Provides investment up to Rs. 50 Lakhs for market entry, commercialization, or scaling up through convertible debentures or debt-linked instruments.

Name: Support for International Patent Protection in E&IT (SIP-EIT)
SubHead: A scheme to provide financial support to MSMEs and Technology Startup units for international patent filing.
Desc:
To provide financial support to MSMEs and Technology Startup units for international patent filing to encourage innovation and recognize the value and capabilities of global IP along with capturing growth opportunities in the ICTE sector.

Provides reimbursement of up to Rs. 15 lakhs per invention or 50% of the total expenses incurred, whichever is less. The support is provided for filing patents in foreign countries.

Name: Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) Scheme
SubHead: One of the flagship programmes of the Government of India for early childhood care and development.
Desc:
To improve the nutritional and health status of children in the age-group 0-6 years; to lay the foundation for proper psychological, physical and social development of the child; to reduce the incidence of mortality, morbidity, malnutrition and school dropout; and to enhance the capability of the mother to look after the normal health and nutritional needs of the child.

The scheme provides a package of six services: supplementary nutrition, pre-school non-formal education, nutrition & health education, immunization, health check-up and referral services. These services are provided through Anganwadi Centres.

Name: National AYUSH Mission (NAM)
SubHead: A mission to promote the AYUSH medical systems through cost-effective AYUSH services.
Desc:
To promote the AYUSH medical systems through cost-effective AYUSH services, strengthening of educational systems, facilitate the enforcement of quality control of Ayurveda, Siddha and Unani & Homoeopathy (ASU & H) drugs and sustainable availability of ASU & H raw-materials.

Supports the upgradation of AYUSH hospitals and dispensaries, setting up of integrated AYUSH hospitals, and cultivation of medicinal plants.

Name: Scheme for providing quality education in Madrasas (SPQEM)
SubHead: A scheme to encourage traditional institutions like Madrasas and Maktabs to introduce modern education.
Desc:
To provide financial assistance to encourage traditional institutions like Madrasas and Maktabs to introduce modern subjects like Science, Mathematics, Social Studies, Hindi and English in their curriculum so that children studying in these institutions can attain academic proficiency for higher studies.

Provides financial assistance for appointing teachers for modern subjects, for their training, for science/computer labs, and for strengthening libraries.

Name: Pradhan Mantri Vaya Vandana Yojana (PMVVY)
SubHead: A pension scheme for senior citizens that provides an assured pension for 10 years.
Desc:
To provide social security during old age and protect elderly persons aged 60 years and above against a future fall in their interest income due to uncertain market conditions.

Provides an assured return of 7.4% p.a. payable monthly for 10 years. Pension is payable at the end of each period, during the policy term of 10 years, as per the frequency of monthly, quarterly, half-yearly, yearly as chosen by the pensioner at the time of purchase.

Name: National Mission on Education through Information and Communication Technology (NMEICT)
SubHead: A mission to leverage the potential of ICT in teaching and learning process for the benefit of all the learners in Higher Education institutions.
Desc:
To leverage the potential of ICT, in teaching and learning process for the benefit of all the learners in Higher Education institutions in any time any where mode. It aims to provide high-quality e-content and connectivity to institutions.

Has led to the development of platforms like SWAYAM (for MOOCs), National Digital Library of India (NDL), and e-Yantra (for robotics). It also provides connectivity to universities under the NKN.

Name: Atal Innovation Mission (AIM)
SubHead: A flagship initiative to promote a culture of innovation and entrepreneurship in the country.
Desc:
To create and promote a culture of innovation and entrepreneurship across the length and breadth of our country. AIM's initiatives include establishing Atal Tinkering Laboratories (ATLs) in schools and Atal Incubation Centers (AICs) in universities and industries.

Provides grants to schools to set up ATLs. Provides financial support to institutions to establish AICs. Organizes challenges and programs to foster innovation.

Name: National Livestock Mission
SubHead: A mission to ensure quantitative and qualitative improvement in livestock production systems and capacity building of all stakeholders.
Desc:
To ensure quantitative and qualitative improvement in livestock production systems and capacity building of all stakeholders. The mission is organized into four sub-missions: Sub-Mission on Fodder and Feed Development, Sub-Mission on Livestock Development, Sub-Mission on Pig Development in North-Eastern Region, and Sub-Mission on Skill Development, Technology Transfer and Extension.

Provides subsidies and support for breed improvement, feed and fodder development, and skill development in the livestock sector.

Name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan (PM-KUSUM)
SubHead: A scheme to promote the use of solar energy in the agriculture sector.
Desc:
To provide energy security to farmers in India, along with honouring India’s commitment to increase the share of installed capacity of electric power from non-fossil-fuel sources. The scheme has three components: setting up of decentralized grid-connected solar power plants, installation of standalone solar-powered agriculture pumps, and solarisation of existing grid-connected agriculture pumps.

Provides central financial assistance (CFA) for the installation of solar pumps and setting up of solar power plants on barren/fallow land of farmers.

Name: National Saffai Karamcharis Finance and Development Corporation (NSKFDC) Schemes
SubHead: Provides concessional finance for income generating activities for Safai Karamcharis, Scavengers and their dependants.
Desc:
To promote socio-economic upliftment of Safai Karamcharis, Scavengers and their dependants by providing concessional finance for setting up of income generating activities and for education.

Provides term loans, micro-credit finance, and education loans at concessional interest rates. Also provides skill development training.

Name: One Stop Centre Scheme
SubHead: A scheme to provide integrated support and assistance to women affected by violence.
Desc:
To provide integrated support and assistance under one roof to women affected by violence, both in private and public spaces. The centres provide medical, legal, psychological and counselling support.

Provides a range of services including emergency response and rescue services, medical assistance, assistance in lodging FIR, psycho-social support, counselling, legal aid, and temporary shelter.

Name: Swadhar Greh Scheme
SubHead: A scheme for women in difficult circumstances, providing them with shelter, food, clothing and care.
Desc:
To cater to the primary need of shelter, food, clothing, medical treatment and care of the women in distress and who are without any social and economic support. It also aims to provide them with legal aid and guidance to enable them to take steps for their readjustment in family/society.

Provides temporary residential accommodation along with food, clothing, medical facilities, counselling, legal aid, and vocational training.

Name: National Creche Scheme for the Children of Working Mothers
SubHead: A scheme to provide day-care facilities to children of working mothers.
Desc:
To provide day-care facilities to children (age group of 6 months to 6 years) of working mothers. The scheme aims to provide a safe and stimulating environment for children, enabling women to take up gainful employment.

Provides services like day-care, supplementary nutrition, health check-ups, and pre-school education.

Name: Scheme of Assistance to Disabled Persons for Purchase/Fitting of Aids/Appliances (ADIP Scheme)
SubHead: A scheme to assist disabled persons in procuring durable, sophisticated and scientifically manufactured aids and appliances.
Desc:
To assist the needy disabled persons in procuring durable, sophisticated and scientifically manufactured, modern, standard aids and appliances that can promote their physical, social and psychological rehabilitation, by reducing the effects of disabilities and enhancing their economic potential.

Provides grants for the purchase and fitting of aids and appliances like wheelchairs, crutches, hearing aids, and artificial limbs.

Name: Accessible India Campaign (Sugamya Bharat Abhiyan)
SubHead: A nationwide campaign for achieving universal accessibility for Persons with Disabilities (PwDs).
Desc:
To create a barrier-free environment for Persons with Disabilities in public buildings, transportation systems, and the ICT ecosystem. The campaign aims to enhance the proportion of accessible government buildings, airports, railway stations, and public documents.

Focuses on retrofitting buildings to make them accessible, making public transport accessible, and ensuring websites and public documents are in accessible formats.

Name: National Action Plan for Drug Demand Reduction (NAPDDR)
SubHead: A plan to address the problem of drug abuse through a multi-pronged strategy.
Desc:
To create awareness and educate people about the ill-effects of drug abuse, and to provide a whole range of community-based services for the identification, motivation, counselling, de-addiction, after-care and rehabilitation of individuals affected by drug abuse.

Provides financial assistance to NGOs and other organizations for running Integrated Rehabilitation Centres for Addicts (IRCAs), outreach and drop-in centres, and community-based peer-led intervention programs.

Name: Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY)
SubHead: A scheme to promote organic farming in the country.
Desc:
To promote organic farming in the country through the adoption of the organic village by cluster approach and PGS certification. The scheme aims to produce agricultural products free from chemicals and pesticides residues.

Provides financial assistance to farmers for various components like mobilization of farmers, PGS certification, and procurement of organic inputs. Farmers are encouraged to form clusters of 50 acres.

Name: e-NAM (National Agriculture Market)
SubHead: A pan-India electronic trading portal for agricultural commodities.
Desc:
To create a unified national market for agricultural commodities by networking existing APMC mandis. It aims to promote better price discovery and provide farmers with more marketing opportunities.

Provides a single window service for all APMC-related information and services. Facilitates transparent online trading with real-time price discovery. Enables farmers to sell their produce to a larger pool of buyers.

Name: Pradhan Mantri Annadata Aay Sanrakshan Abhiyan (PM-AASHA)
SubHead: An umbrella scheme aimed at ensuring remunerative prices to farmers for their produce.
Desc:
To ensure that farmers get remunerative prices for their produce as announced in the Union Budget. The scheme is comprised of three sub-schemes: Price Support Scheme (PSS), Price Deficiency Payment Scheme (PDPS), and Private Procurement & Stockist Scheme (PPSS).

Under PSS, central nodal agencies procure pulses, oilseeds, and copra at MSP. Under PDPS, the difference between the MSP and the actual market price is paid directly to the farmer. PPSS allows private agencies to procure at MSP.

Name: National Food Security Act (NFSA), 2013
SubHead: An act to provide for food and nutritional security by ensuring access to adequate quantity of quality food at affordable prices.
Desc:
To provide for food and nutritional security in human life cycle approach, by ensuring access to adequate quantity of quality food at affordable prices to people to live a life with dignity.

Provides subsidized food grains (rice, wheat, coarse grains) to up to 75% of the rural population and 50% of the urban population under the Targeted Public Distribution System (TPDS). It also includes provisions for nutritional support to pregnant women, lactating mothers, and children.

Name: Scheme for Adolescent Girls (SAG)
SubHead: A scheme aimed at empowering out-of-school adolescent girls.
Desc:
To facilitate, educate and empower Adolescent Girls so as to enable them to become self-reliant and aware citizens. The scheme targets out-of-school girls in the age group of 11-14 years.

Provides nutritional support, health check-ups, and life skills education. It also aims to mainstream these girls back into formal schooling or vocational training.

Name: National Career Service (NCS)
SubHead: A one-stop solution that provides a wide array of employment and career-related services to the citizens of India.
Desc:
To provide a national platform for job seekers and employers for job matching. It also provides career counselling and guidance.

Job seekers can register and search for jobs. Employers can register and post job vacancies. The portal also offers information on skill development courses and local service providers.

Name: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY)
SubHead: A scheme to provide loans up to Rs. 10 lakh to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises.
Desc:
To provide funding to the non-corporate, non-farm sector income generating activities of micro and small enterprises whose credit needs are below Rs. 10 Lakh. The loans are given by commercial banks, RRBs, small finance banks, MFIs and NBFCs.

Loans are categorized as Shishu (up to Rs. 50,000), Kishor (Rs. 50,001 to Rs. 5 lakh), and Tarun (Rs. 5,00,001 to Rs. 10 lakh). No collateral is required.

Name: National Urban Livelihoods Mission (NULM)
SubHead: A mission to reduce poverty and vulnerability of the urban poor households.
Desc:
To reduce poverty and vulnerability of the urban poor households by enabling them to access gainful self-employment and skilled wage employment opportunities, resulting in an appreciable improvement in their livelihoods on a sustainable basis.

Focuses on organizing the urban poor into Self-Help Groups (SHGs), providing skill training, setting up individual and group micro-enterprises, and providing shelters for the urban homeless.

Name: Atal Bhujal Yojana (ATAL JAL)
SubHead: A central sector scheme for sustainable management of ground water resources with community participation.
Desc:
To improve ground water management through community participation in identified priority areas in seven states, viz. Gujarat, Haryana, Karnataka, Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra, Rajasthan and Uttar Pradesh.

Focuses on demand-side management of water resources. Promotes water use efficiency through micro-irrigation, crop diversification, and preparation of Water Security Plans by Gram Panchayats.

Name: FAME India Scheme (Faster Adoption and Manufacturing of Electric Vehicles)
SubHead: A scheme to promote the manufacturing and adoption of electric and hybrid vehicles.
Desc:
To promote the manufacturing of electric and hybrid vehicle technology and to ensure sustainable growth of the same. The scheme is implemented in phases, with Phase-II focusing on supporting the electrification of public and shared transportation.

Provides demand incentives to buyers of electric vehicles in the form of an upfront reduced price. Also supports the creation of charging infrastructure.

Name: National Bamboo Mission (NBM)
SubHead: A mission to increase the area under bamboo plantation in non-forest Government and private lands.
Desc:
To increase the area under bamboo plantation in non-forest Government and private lands to supplement farm income and contribute to the resilience of the agriculture sector. It also aims to promote the marketing of bamboo and bamboo-based handicrafts.

Provides subsidies for raising high-yielding bamboo plantations. Supports the establishment of bamboo treatment plants, product development, and marketing.

Name: Special Central Assistance to Scheduled Castes Sub-Plan (SCA to SCSP)
SubHead: A scheme to provide additional funding to states for the economic development of Scheduled Caste persons.
Desc:
To give a thrust to the development programmes for Scheduled Castes by providing financial assistance to States/UTs as an additive to their Scheduled Caste Sub-Plan (SCSP). The assistance is primarily for income-generating economic development schemes.

Funds are used to fill the critical gaps in financial and physical terms to ensure that the schemes have a larger and more viable coverage. It supports family-oriented income generating schemes and infrastructure development in SC-dominated villages.

Name: Pradhan Mantri Adarsh Gram Yojana (PMAGY)
SubHead: A scheme for the integrated development of villages with more than 50% Scheduled Caste population.
Desc:
To ensure integrated development of the selected villages with more than 50% SC population into 'Adarsh Grams' (Model Villages). The development is achieved through the convergence of central and state schemes and the provision of 'Gap-filling' funds.

Focuses on improving infrastructure and socio-economic indicators in the selected villages across domains like drinking water, sanitation, education, health, and social security.

Name: National Overseas Scholarship Scheme
SubHead: A scheme to facilitate low-income students belonging to the SC, ST, and other specified categories to obtain higher education abroad.
Desc:
To facilitate the low-income students belonging to the Scheduled Castes, Denotified Nomadic and Semi-Nomadic Tribes, Landless Agricultural Labourers and Traditional Artisans category to obtain higher education viz., Master degree or Ph.D courses by studying abroad.

Provides financial assistance for tuition fees, maintenance allowance, contingency allowance, and travel expenses.

Name: Free Coaching Scheme for SC and OBC Students
SubHead: A scheme to provide good quality coaching for economically disadvantaged SC and OBC candidates.
Desc:
To provide good quality coaching for economically disadvantaged Scheduled Castes (SCs) and Other Backward Classes (OBCs) candidates to enable them to appear in competitive examinations and succeed in obtaining an appropriate job in Public/Private sector.

The scheme provides financial assistance to reputed coaching institutions to provide coaching for competitive exams like Civil Services, SSC, Banking, Railways, and entrance exams for professional courses like engineering and medicine.

Name: National Fellowship for Scheduled Caste Students
SubHead: A fellowship for SC students to pursue higher studies such as M.Phil. and Ph.D.
Desc:
To provide fellowships in the form of financial assistance to students belonging to the SC category to pursue higher studies such as M.Phil. and Ph.D. in Sciences, Humanities and Social Sciences in Indian Universities/Institutions/Colleges recognized by UGC.

Provides a monthly fellowship amount, contingency grant, and other allowances as per UGC norms.

Name: Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Janaushadhi Pariyojana (PMBJP)
SubHead: A campaign launched to provide quality medicines at affordable prices to the masses.
Desc:
To make quality generic medicines available at affordable prices for all, particularly the poor and disadvantaged, through exclusive outlets 'Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Janaushadhi Kendras'.

Provides generic medicines at much lower prices compared to branded alternatives, reducing out-of-pocket expenditure on healthcare.

Name: Senior Citizen's Welfare Fund
SubHead: A fund to be utilized for schemes for the promotion of the welfare of senior citizens.
Desc:
To support financial security, healthcare, nutrition, shelter, welfare and other needs of senior citizens. The fund is created from the unclaimed deposits in small savings schemes, PPF, and EPF accounts.

The fund is used to provide financial assistance to projects and schemes that benefit senior citizens, such as the Rashtriya Vayoshri Yojana and schemes for setting up old age homes.

Name: Rashtriya Vayoshri Yojana (RVY)
SubHead: A scheme for providing Physical Aids and Assisted-living Devices for Senior citizens belonging to BPL category.
Desc:
To provide senior citizens, belonging to BPL category and suffering from any of the age-related disability/infirmity, with such assisted-living devices which can restore near normalcy in their bodily functions.

Provides devices like walking sticks, elbow crutches, walkers, hearing aids, wheelchairs, and artificial dentures free of cost.

Name: National Programme for the Health Care of Elderly (NPHCE)
SubHead: A program to provide accessible, affordable, and high-quality long-term, comprehensive and dedicated care services to an Ageing population.
Desc:
To provide dedicated health care facilities to the elderly people through State Health society at various level of state health care delivery system i.e. PHC, CHC, District Hospitals.

Provides dedicated OPD services, in-patient wards, and rehabilitation services for the elderly at various levels of the public health system. It also supports home-based care for bedridden elderly.

Name: Ude Desh ka Aam Naagrik (UDAN)
SubHead: A regional airport development and 'Regional Connectivity Scheme' (RCS) of Government of India.
Desc:
To make air travel affordable and widespread, to boost inclusive national economic development, job growth and air transport infrastructure development of all regions and states of India. It aims to connect un-served and under-served airports.

Capped airfares on regional routes to make flying affordable for the common man. Viability Gap Funding (VGF) is provided to airline operators to encourage them to fly on regional routes.

Name: Sagarmala Programme
SubHead: A programme to promote port-led development in the country.
Desc:
To promote port-led development in the country by harnessing India's 7,500 km long coastline, 14,500 km of potentially navigable waterways and strategic location on key international maritime trade routes. The four pillars are Port Modernization, Port Connectivity, Port-led Industrialization, and Coastal Community Development.

Focuses on reducing logistics costs for EXIM and domestic trade. Develops infrastructure like new ports, modernization of existing ports, and improving road/rail connectivity to ports. It also includes projects for the development of coastal communities.

Name: National Clean Air Programme (NCAP)
SubHead: A national level strategy to tackle the air pollution problem across the country in a comprehensive manner.
Desc:
To achieve a national-level target of 20%–30% reduction of PM2.5 and PM10 concentration by 2024, keeping 2017 as the base year for the comparison of concentration. The programme focuses on 122 non-attainment cities.

City-specific action plans are being implemented to strengthen the air quality monitoring network, reduce vehicular and industrial emissions, and increase public awareness.

Name: Namami Gange Programme
SubHead: An Integrated Conservation Mission to accomplish the twin objectives of effective abatement of pollution, conservation and rejuvenation of National River Ganga.
Desc:
To accomplish the twin objectives of effective abatement of pollution, conservation and rejuvenation of National River Ganga. The main pillars are sewerage treatment infrastructure, river-front development, river-surface cleaning, biodiversity conservation, afforestation, and public awareness.

Focuses on creating sewage treatment capacity, developing river ghats, cleaning the river surface, and promoting biodiversity and afforestation along the river.

Name: Pradhan Mantri Laghu Vyapari Maan-dhan Yojana (PM-LVM)
SubHead: A pension scheme for small shopkeepers, retail traders, and self-employed persons.
Desc:
To provide old age protection and social security of small-scale traders and retailers. It is a voluntary and contributory pension scheme.

Provides a minimum assured pension of Rs. 3000 per month on attaining the age of 60 years. The central government makes a matching contribution to the subscriber's account.

Name: Atal Beemit Vyakti Kalyan Yojana (ABVKY)
SubHead: A welfare measure for employees covered under the ESI Act, providing unemployment benefits.
Desc:
To provide relief in the form of cash compensation to Insured Persons (IPs) who become unemployed. It provides relief for a certain period while they search for new engagement.

Provides cash compensation to the extent of 50% of the average per day earning of the insured person for a maximum period of 90 days of unemployment.

Name: National Child Labour Project (NCLP) Scheme
SubHead: A scheme for the rehabilitation of child labour.
Desc:
To withdraw children working in hazardous occupations and processes and to mainstream them into the formal education system. The scheme focuses on providing bridging education, vocational training, and other support to rescued child labourers.

Children rescued from work are enrolled in NCLP Special Training Centres, where they are provided with bridging education, vocational training, mid-day meal, stipend, and health care.

Name: Mahila Shakti Kendra (MSK) Scheme
SubHead: A scheme to empower rural women through community participation.
Desc:
To empower rural women through community participation and to create an environment in which they realize their full potential. The scheme aims to provide an interface for rural women to approach the government for availing their entitlements.

Establishes Mahila Shakti Kendras at the village, block, and district levels. Student volunteers engage with the community to create awareness about government schemes and programs for women.

Name: Mahila Police Volunteers (MPV) Scheme
SubHead: A scheme to create a link between the police and the community to help women in distress.
Desc:
To create a link between the police and the community and facilitate women in distress. MPVs act as a public-police interface and report incidences of violence against women, such as domestic violence, child marriage, dowry harassment, and violence faced by women in public spaces.

MPVs are not police officers but serve as a bridge to the formal police system. They provide information to women about their rights and the services available to them.

Name: Nirbhaya Fund
SubHead: A dedicated fund for implementation of initiatives aimed at enhancing the safety and security for women in the country.
Desc:
To support initiatives by the government and NGOs working towards protecting the dignity and ensuring the safety of women in India. The fund is used for projects specifically designed to improve the safety and security of women.

Has funded various projects including the One Stop Centre Scheme, Women Helplines, and the Emergency Response Support System (ERSS) which provides a single emergency number (112).

Name: National Mission for a Green India (GIM)
SubHead: One of the eight Missions under the National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC).
Desc:
To protect, restore and enhance India’s forest cover and to respond to climate change. The mission aims at increasing forest/tree cover on 5 million hectares of forest/non-forest lands and improving the quality of forest cover on another 5 million hectares.

Focuses on afforestation, restoration of degraded forests, and improving forest-based livelihood income for about 3 million households.

Name: National Cyclone Risk Mitigation Project (NCRMP)
SubHead: A project to undertake suitable structural and non-structural measures to mitigate the effects of cyclones in the coastal states and UTs of India.
Desc:
To minimize vulnerability to cyclones and make people and infrastructure disaster resilient. The project focuses on constructing multi-purpose cyclone shelters, access roads, and improving the early warning dissemination system.

Construction of cyclone shelters provides a safe place for people during cyclones. Improved warning systems help in timely evacuation.

Name: e-Courts Mission Mode Project
SubHead: A project to provide efficient and time-bound citizen-centric services through ICT enablement of courts.
Desc:
To provide designated services to litigants, lawyers and the judiciary through ICT enablement of courts. The project aims to make the justice delivery system more efficient and transparent.

Provides services like online case status, cause lists, and court orders. It has enabled e-filing of cases and virtual court hearings.

Name: Gram Ujala Scheme
SubHead: A scheme to offer high-quality LED bulbs at an affordable price in rural areas.
Desc:
To provide high-quality LED bulbs at an affordable price of Rs. 10 per bulb in rural areas. The scheme aims to promote energy efficiency and reduce electricity bills for rural consumers.

Rural consumers can exchange their working incandescent bulbs for high-quality LED bulbs at a nominal price. This leads to significant energy and cost savings.

Name: Saubhagya Scheme (Pradhan Mantri Sahaj Bijli Har Ghar Yojana)
SubHead: A scheme to achieve universal household electrification in the country.
Desc:
To achieve universal household electrification by providing electricity connections to all un-electrified households in rural areas and all poor households in urban areas in the country.

Provides free electricity connections to all poor households. Other households are charged a nominal fee.

Name: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Gram Jyoti Yojana (DDUGJY)
SubHead: A scheme for rural electrification, focusing on feeder separation and strengthening of sub-transmission and distribution infrastructure.
Desc:
To provide continuous power supply to rural India. The scheme focuses on feeder separation (rural households & agricultural) and strengthening of sub-transmission and distribution infrastructure including metering at all levels in rural areas.

Improves the quality and reliability of power supply in rural areas. Reduces transmission losses.

Name: National Programme on Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Storage
SubHead: A Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme to promote the manufacturing of ACC battery storage in India.
Desc:
To achieve a manufacturing capacity of 50 GWh of ACC and 5 GWh of 'Niche' ACC with an outlay of Rs. 18,100 crore. The scheme aims to reduce import dependence and promote domestic manufacturing of batteries.

Provides a production-linked incentive to selected manufacturers of ACC batteries. This is expected to attract large-scale investment in the battery manufacturing sector.

Name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Large Scale Electronics Manufacturing
SubHead: A scheme to boost domestic manufacturing and attract large investments in the electronics value chain.
Desc:
To boost domestic manufacturing and attract large investments in the electronics value chain including electronic components and semiconductor packaging. The scheme focuses on mobile phone manufacturing and specified electronic components.

Provides an incentive of 4% to 6% on incremental sales (over base year) of goods manufactured in India and covered under target segments, to eligible companies, for a period of five years.

Name: Modified Special Incentive Package Scheme (M-SIPS)
SubHead: A scheme to provide a special incentive package to promote large-scale manufacturing in the Electronic System Design and Manufacturing (ESDM) sector.
Desc:
To offset disability and attract investments in the ESDM sector. The scheme provides capital subsidy for investments in the electronics manufacturing sector.

Provides a subsidy of 20-25% on capital expenditure for setting up an ESDM unit. The scheme also provides for reimbursement of CVD/excise on capital equipment for non-SEZ units.

Name: Electronics Manufacturing Clusters (EMC) Scheme
SubHead: A scheme to provide support for the creation of world-class infrastructure for electronics manufacturing.
Desc:
To provide support for the creation of world-class infrastructure along with common facilities and amenities for attracting investments in the ESDM sector. The scheme supports both Greenfield and Brownfield EMCs.

Provides financial assistance for setting up of Electronics Manufacturing Clusters. The assistance is in the form of a grant-in-aid.

Name: SWADESH Darshan Scheme
SubHead: An integrated theme-based tourism circuit development scheme.
Desc:
To develop theme-based tourist circuits in the country. These circuits are developed on the principles of high tourist value, competitiveness and sustainability in an integrated manner.

The scheme provides central financial assistance to State Governments/UT Administrations for the development of infrastructure in the identified circuits, such as Buddhist Circuit, Coastal Circuit, Himalayan Circuit, etc.

Name: National Mission on Pilgrimage Rejuvenation and Spiritual, Heritage Augmentation Drive (PRASHAD)
SubHead: A scheme focused on developing and identifying pilgrimage sites across India for enriching the religious tourism experience.
Desc:
To facilitate the integrated development of pilgrimage destinations in a planned, prioritized and sustainable manner to provide a complete religious tourism experience.

Provides financial assistance for the development of infrastructure at identified pilgrimage sites, such as entry points, last-mile connectivity, and basic tourist facilities.

Name: Scheme of Financial Assistance for Promotion of Art and Culture
SubHead: An umbrella scheme with various sub-schemes to provide financial support to artists, scholars, and cultural organizations.
Desc:
To support and promote the art and culture of the country. The scheme has components for providing financial assistance to professional groups and individuals for specified performing arts projects, and for the preservation of cultural heritage.

Provides grants to cultural organizations, scholarships to young artists, and fellowships to outstanding persons in the field of culture.

Name: Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship for College and University Students
SubHead: A scholarship scheme to provide financial assistance to meritorious students from low-income families.
Desc:
To provide financial assistance to meritorious students from poor families to meet a part of their day-to-day expenses while pursuing higher studies.

Provides a scholarship of Rs. 10,000 per annum at the graduation level and Rs. 20,000 per annum at the post-graduation level.

Name: Prime Minister's Research Fellows (PMRF) Scheme
SubHead: A scheme to attract the talent pool of the country to doctoral (Ph.D.) programs for carrying out research in cutting edge science and technology domains.
Desc:
To attract the talent pool of the country to doctoral (Ph.D.) programs for carrying out research in cutting edge science and technology domains, with a focus on national priorities.

Provides a handsome fellowship for a period of five years, along with a research grant.

Name: Visvesvaraya PhD Scheme for Electronics and IT
SubHead: A scheme to enhance the number of PhDs in the country in the ESDM and IT/ITES sectors.
Desc:
To enhance the number of PhDs in the country in the Electronics System Design & Manufacturing (ESDM) and IT/IT Enabled Services (IT/ITES) sectors. The scheme aims to create a pool of PhDs to meet the growing needs of the industry and academia.

Provides fellowships to full-time PhD candidates. Also provides an infrastructure grant to the academic institution.

Name: Interest Subvention Scheme for MSMEs
SubHead: A scheme to provide interest subvention of 2% on loans to all GST registered MSMEs.
Desc:
To encourage MSMEs to get registered under the GST regime and to enhance their access to credit. The scheme provides an interest subvention of 2% on fresh or incremental loans.

Provides an interest relief of 2% per annum to eligible MSMEs on their outstanding fresh/incremental term loan/working capital.

Name: Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP)
SubHead: A credit-linked subsidy programme for generating employment opportunities through the establishment of micro-enterprises.
Desc:
To generate employment opportunities in rural as well as urban areas through setting up of new self-employment ventures/projects/micro enterprises.

Provides a subsidy on the project cost for setting up a new micro-enterprise. The subsidy amount varies based on the category of the beneficiary and the location of the unit (urban/rural).

Name: A Scheme for Promotion of Innovation, Rural Industries and Entrepreneurship (ASPIRE)
SubHead: A scheme to set up a network of technology centers and to set up incubation centers to accelerate entrepreneurship.
Desc:
To set up a network of technology centers and to set up incubation centers to accelerate entrepreneurship and also to promote startups for innovation and entrepreneurship in the agro-industry.

Provides financial support for setting up Livelihood Business Incubators (LBIs) and Technology Business Incubators (TBIs). It also supports projects for innovation and enterprise creation in the agro-based industry.

Name: Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme (CLCSS) for Technology Upgradation
SubHead: A scheme to facilitate technology upgradation in Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs).
Desc:
To facilitate technology upgradation in Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) by providing an upfront capital subsidy of 15 per cent on institutional finance availed by them for induction of well-established and improved technology in specified sub-sectors/products.

Provides a 15% capital subsidy on institutional credit up to Rs. 1 crore for technology upgradation.

Name: National Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation (NSFDC) Schemes
SubHead: Provides concessional finance for income generating activities for Scheduled Caste persons living below the poverty line.
Desc:
To finance, facilitate and mobilize funds for the economic empowerment of persons belonging to the Scheduled Castes living below double the poverty line. It provides financial assistance for income generating schemes.

Provides term loans, micro-credit finance, and education loans at concessional interest rates through State Channelizing Agencies (SCAs).

Name: National Backward Classes Finance and Development Corporation (NBCFDC) Schemes
SubHead: Provides concessional finance for income generating activities for members of Backward Classes.
Desc:
To promote economic and developmental activities for the benefit of Backward Classes and to assist the poorer section of these classes in skill development and self-employment ventures.

Provides term loans and micro-finance at concessional rates of interest to eligible members of Backward Classes for undertaking various income generating activities.

Name: National Handicapped Finance and Development Corporation (NHFDC) Schemes
SubHead: Provides concessional finance for income generating activities for Persons with Disabilities.
Desc:
To promote economic development activities and self-employment ventures for the benefit of Persons with Disabilities. It provides financial assistance at concessional rates.

Provides loans for starting a business, for education, and for purchasing assistive devices. It also supports skill development training.
